title: Coelogyne murudensis
intro: Coelogyne murudensis is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, providing various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the severity of certain respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne murudensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coelogyne murudensis include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Coelogyne murudensis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Coelogyne, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. The species is endemic to Malaysia, specifically in the states of Sabah and Sarawak. Coelogyne murudensis is a terrestrial orchid that grows in the wild in lowland and montane forests. It is listed as Endangered on the IUCN Red List due to habitat loss and degradation.
